topic.what: Co-creation of LLM-based companion tools for SOC analysts through embedded ethnography
topic.how: Six-month researcher embedding with participant observation and iterative tool development guided by Nonaka's SECI model
topic.why_matters: Addresses persistent gap between academic SOC research and operational practice by involving practitioners in tool design

characteristics.what_it_measures: Sustained tool usage after fieldwork conclusion
characteristics.vs_baseline: Initial skepticism transformed to sustained adoption
characteristics.evidence: P7: tool used regularly; P5: adopted as on-demand resource
characteristics.context: Self-reported by 18 SOC analysts over 6 months

characteristics.what_it_measures: Perceived reduction in analyst mental effort
characteristics.vs_baseline: Previously high cognitive strain from distributed data
characteristics.evidence: P5: helps with things they dont know; P11: conversations should be happening
characteristics.context: Analyst feedback on distributed data consolidation

characteristics.what_itasures: Time to perform root cause analysis
characteristics.evidence: P7: RCA tool saves a lot of time; starting investigations with RCA approach

characteristics.what_it_measures: Correct agent/tool selection by LLM platform
characteristics.vs_baseline: Variability between Mistral and Llama models
characteristics.evidence: Four models tested with five representative queries; GPT-OSS 20B exceeded runtime limit
characteristics.context: Controlled evaluation of Llama 3.2 3B, Mistral 7B, Qwen3 8B, GPT-OSS 20B

characteristics.value: Institutional trust transferred to direct analyst engagement
characteristics.vs_baseline: Initial skepticism requiring internal advocate mediation
characteristics.evidence: IA role enabled access; over time direct engagement indicated transferred trust

novelty: LLM-Oriented SECI extension with generative-recombination and trust-calibrated-internalization
Six-month embedded ethnography with co-created LLM tools in live SOC
Three LLM companion tools (RCA, Query Builder, Asset Discovery) via practitioner feedback
Demonstration that sociotechnical co-creation overcomes traditional SOC adoption barriers
open_problems: Multi-site longitudinal studies across diverse SOC environments
Impact of organizational maturity on trust calibration dynamics
Generalizability of LLM-Oriented SECI beyond single-organization setting
Evolution of adoption dynamics as LLM capabilities and governance frameworks mature
